After carefully reviewing the newly acquired evidence relating specifically to the Bluegrass appliance and comparing it against existing knowledge about similar orthodontic devices, I concur that the correct answer remains encompassing both thumb sucking and tongue thrusting as primary areas addressed by such appliances.
My analysis incorporates observations that several orthodontic methods target muscle functions contributing to misalignment—whether those involve thumbs in mouths or tongues pressing forward during swallowing. Therefore, acknowledging both conditions under consideration enhances accuracy reflecting real-world application of these devices.
Thus, continuing support for Option C (\boxed{C}) stands justified despite slight nuances introduced by varied terminology utilized interchangeably among practitioners.
